bat the village issued 240 tokens in one year, each with a value of 3 pence. the average purchase cost was 18 pence, so the total value of the tokens was 240 × 3 = 720 pence. the total amount of money available was 720 pence, and each purchase cost 18 pence, so the number of purchases was 720 ÷ 18 = 40. the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year.

the historian found that the village used these tokens to pay for goods, with an average purchase costing 18 pence. the total number of tokens issued was 240, and each token was worth 3 pence, so the total value was 240 × 3 = 720 pence. the total amount of money available was 720 pence, and each purchase cost 18 pence, so the number of purchases was 720 ÷ 18 = 40. the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year.
